sanskrit: तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः । पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु ऐसे पाँच हेतुओंके होनेपर भी जो उस कर्मोंके विषयमें केवल शुद्ध आत्माको कर्ता मानता है, वह दुर्मति ठीक नहीं समझता क्योंकि उसकी बुद्धि शुद्ध नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: But one who sees only the pure self as the doer, even though these five causes are present, has not seen rightly; such a confused mind does not understand.
essence: Action has many causes; the pure self is not the lone doer.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Action does not come from the pure self alone. A confused person wrongly thinks the self is the only doer and misses the real causes at work.
simpleInsight: You can stop carrying the whole burden of authorship. Clear seeing brings relief from false responsibility.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Krishna is dismantling Arjuna's confusion about action. After naming the five causes behind every deed, he warns that a person with muddled understanding wrongly blames or claims the pure self as the sole doer.
modernRelevance: You replay a conversation at 1 a.m. and decide, "I caused everything." Krishna cuts through that mental trap: one person is rarely the whole cause.
translationLiteral: तत्र (there) / एवम् (thus) / सति (being present) / कर्तारम् (the doer) / आत्मानम् (the self) / केवलम् (only) / तु (but) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / अकृतबुद्धित्वात् (because of unformed understanding) / न (not) / सः (he) / पश्यति (sees) / दुर्मतिः (confused-minded one)
insight: It shifts the mind from personal overclaiming to clear seeing of shared causes.

quoteVariants.text: Five causes act. The pure self is not the lone doer.
The self is not the only doer.
I keep calling myself the doer, but five forces are at work.
See action clearly. Drop false authorship.
